concept: State / Relevant Conditions of a System at a Time
description: The selected conditions of a system at a given time. A state is a chosen description built for a purpose, never the whole truth of the thing it describes, and a different question can select different conditions to describe.
sound_symbolism: 'Mi' gathers the selected properties and conditions, 're' relates them within one analytical description, and open 'la' lays the snapshot out for comparison. The ending leaves room for the state to change and for a different model to select different variables.
grammatical_notes: A noun commonly possessed by the system or component described: 'ha terura mirela mureo nai.' (This system's state is stable.) Use time expressions and evidentials to identify when and how the state is known. Distinct from 'norelu' (form), 'noa' (position), and a temporary adjective used without an analytical state description.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Tracking system states supports transparent maintenance and adaptation while exposing which conditions a design chooses to notice.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A state is presented as a conditioned description at a time, not as a permanent essence.

tags.cognition: a selected description used to analyze change
tags.physical: the term can describe measurable material and operational conditions
tags.temporal: conditions attributed to a particular time
